methods.description: An experimental three-component mixture design was performed to evaluate the individual and combined bioactivity effects of three polyphenolic extracts against six bacteria. The design included 13 runs and used a complete cubic model to analyze the effects.

steps.description: An experimental three-component mixture design was performed using the software Statgraphics Centurion XVI (StatPoint-Technologies S.A, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform mixture design using Statgraphics Centurion XVI

steps.description: The design included 13 runs that evaluate the individual and combined bioactivity effects of 3 polyphenolic extracts against six bacteria.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate bioactivity effects of extracts against bacteria

steps.description: The complete cubic model was applied for three components: where P, Q and U represent the extracts.
steps.searchable_summary: apply complete cubic model to three components

steps.description: Y is the measured response (i.e. total phenolic content, total flavonoid content or antibacterial activity against each strain, βi's are the estimated coefficients for each linear term, and δij's are the cross product of the model.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze measured response using model coefficients

steps.description: The sign and the magnitude value of the β coefficients indicate the type of effect (i.e., additive, synergistic or antagonistic).
steps.searchable_summary: interpret β coefficients to determine effect type
